# ARC-QUIC-RFC9001-0002 - QUIC RFC 9001 TLS KeyUpdate Classifier Architecture

## Purpose

Describe the narrow post-handshake TLS KeyUpdate classifier seam that turns prohibited TLS KeyUpdate messages into the existing KeyUpdateError close path without widening into general post-handshake TLS support.

## Requirements Satisfied

- REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6-0009
- REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6-0010

## Design Summary

The transcript owner classifies post-handshake TLS bytes before bridge publication. A TLS KeyUpdate message is recognized as a dedicated post-handshake classifier outcome, published through the existing prohibited-update path, and then closed by QuicConnectionRuntime as KeyUpdateError. The internal QuicTlsUpdateKind.KeyUpdateInstalled and key-phase install path remain separate so managed QUIC key updates do not masquerade as TLS KeyUpdate support.

## Key Components

- src/Incursa.Quic/QuicTlsTransport.cs
- src/Incursa.Quic/QuicTlsTranscriptProgress.cs
- src/Incursa.Quic/QuicTlsTransportBridgeDriver.cs
- src/Incursa.Quic/QuicTransportTlsBridgeState.cs
- src/Incursa.Quic/QuicConnectionRuntime.cs
- tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/RequirementHomes/RFC9001/REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6-0009.cs
- tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/RequirementHomes/RFC9001/REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6-0010.cs

## Edge Cases and Constraints

- Only the TLS KeyUpdate post-handshake shape is classified here.
- NewSessionTicket remains the only supported positive post-handshake publication path in this slice.
- Internal QUIC key-phase successor installation is not a TLS KeyUpdate and must stay on the separate QuicTlsUpdateKind.KeyUpdateInstalled path.

## Risks

- If the classifier starts handling unrelated post-handshake TLS messages, the RFC 9001 proof surface will widen beyond this lane.
- If the key-phase install path is conflated with TLS KeyUpdate handling, the prohibition proof will become ambiguous.
